claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age:**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child (or children, if you have more than one you'd like to claim for).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for that information! Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), that's a great start for eligibility.

Let me navigate to the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?

Since Alex is only 8 years old, I expect the answer is **yes** - they're not working, or at least not working 24+ hours per week. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex doesn't work at all. He's only 8, so he's just at primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense! Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not** receiving certain benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, since Alex is only 8 years old, I'd expect he's not claiming any benefits in his own name. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving ESA or Universal Credit in his own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a child.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming! Let's move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently on an apprenticeship?

Since Alex is 8 years old and in primary school, I assume he's **not** in any apprenticeship. Can you confirm that Alex is not doing an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just 8 and in primary school like I mentioned.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You live in the UK as your main home
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (and not claiming certain benefits like Income Support or Universal Credit)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Do you normally live in the UK, or does any of the other options apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you might still qualify through the Crown servant route.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people like:
- UK government employees posted overseas
- Diplomatic staff
- Armed forces personnel stationed abroad

Do you fall into this category, or are you living abroad for other reasons?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad for other reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, I've reached an outcome for your Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm afraid you are **not currently eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 8 | ❌ No | Claimant does not meet UK residency requirements |

### Explanation

While Alex meets all the criteria for being a qualifying child:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in his own right
- ✅ Not in an apprenticeship

Unfortunately, **you** (as the claimant) do not meet the UK residency requirements because:
- ❌ You don't normally live in the UK
- ❌ You're not a Crown servant posted abroad
- ❌ You don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

### What You Can Do

If your circumstances change in the future, you may become eligible. For example:
- If you **return to live in the UK**, you could then apply for Child Benefit
- If you become a **Crown servant** posted abroad, you may qualify

For more information, you can visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I understand this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which meet the child eligibility criteria. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not normally live in the UK. Therefore, despite Alex being a qualifying child, the claimant cannot claim Child Benefit due to not meeting the residency requirements.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While Alex qualifies as an eligible child (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet specific circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status or being a Crown servant abroad. Since the claimant lives abroad for non-Crown servant reasons, they do not satisfy the residency criteria.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

Therefore, despite Alex being a qualifying child, the claimant cannot claim Child Benefit due to not meeting the residency requirements.

Since the claimant lives abroad for non-Crown servant reasons, they do not satisfy the residency criteria.
